items.headline: Council to discuss three lawsuits against Cupertino in closed session
items.summary: City Council is scheduled to meet with legal counsel on pending litigation including cases brought by Lixin (Leon) Chen, Yes in My Backyard, and the Garden Gate Coalition for Mary Avenue Safety against the City of Cupertino, all filed in Santa Clara County Superior Court.

items.headline: $5.4M interior tenant improvement permitted at 488 Almaden Blvd
items.summary: A building permit for interior tenant improvement work at 488 Almaden Boulevard carries a $5,427,195 valuation, one of the largest permits in this batch. No tenant or business name is listed in the permit description.

items.headline: Two detached ADUs permitted on same Lewis Road lot
items.summary: 458 Lewis Rd received permits for two separate detached one-story ADUs (units #5 and #6) on the same multi-family lot, each valued around $131,764, adding rental-style units without new principal construction.

items.headline: Bank building could become 110-unit affordable housing complex
items.summary: Planning Commission is holding a study session on a proposal to demolish an existing 10,038-square-foot bank building and combine four parcels to build a five-story development with 109 affordable units plus one manager's unit over ground-floor parking.
